name: Seila Law LLC v. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
summary: Held that the CFPB's single-director structure with for-cause removal protection violated separation of powers, while distinguishing and not overruling Humphrey's Executor's holding about multi-member commissions.
significance: Both sides relied heavily on this case: petitioners cited its reasoning about executive power and removal authority; respondents cited its distinction between single-director and multi-member agencies.
